---
output: github_document
---

<!-- BENCHMARK-RESULT.md is generated — do not edit by hand.
     Re-render with:
       Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("benchmarks/BENCHMARK-RESULT.Rmd")'
     Or run the full pipeline via benchmarks/run.sh
     Methodology, caveats, and reproduction instructions live in README.md. -->

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      results = "asis")
library(arrow)
library(knitr)

# Rmd is in benchmarks/; results/ is a sibling. knitr's default wd
# is the Rmd's directory, so these resolve correctly.
paths <- list(
  timing = function(p) sprintf("results/latest/%s-timing.parquet", p),
  equiv  = "results/latest/equivalence.parquet"
)

read_timing <- function(p) {
  path <- paths$timing(p)
  if (!file.exists(path)) return(NULL)
  as.data.frame(arrow::read_parquet(path))
}

t_hdf5r  <- read_timing("hdf5r")
t_sl     <- read_timing("spacelaser")
t_h5coro <- read_timing("h5coro")
t_gedidb <- read_timing("gedidb")

equiv <- if (file.exists(paths$equiv))
  as.data.frame(arrow::read_parquet(paths$equiv)) else
  data.frame()

fmt_s  <- function(x) if (is.na(x)) "—" else sprintf("%.1f s", x)
fmt_b  <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x)) "—"
  else format(structure(x, class = "object_size"), units = "auto")
}
fmt_int <- function(x) if (is.na(x)) "—" else format(x, big.mark = ",")
```

# Benchmark result

```{r provenance}
frames <- Filter(Negate(is.null), list(t_hdf5r, t_sl, t_h5coro, t_gedidb))
if (length(frames) == 0L) {
  cat("*No benchmark data yet. Run `benchmarks/run.sh`.*\n")
} else {
  row <- frames[[1L]][1L, ]
  cat(sprintf(
    "GEDI **%s**, bbox `%s`, %s → %s, %s granules. Run at **%s**.\n",
    row$product, row$bbox, row$date_start, row$date_end,
    fmt_int(row$n_granules), row$timestamp
  ))
}
have_data <- length(frames) > 0L
```

## Headline

```{r headline, eval = have_data}
pipelines <- list(
  list(label = "Spacelaser (Rust, partial HTTP range reads)", t = t_sl),
  list(label = "h5coro (Python, partial HTTP range reads)",    t = t_h5coro),
  list(label = "Download + hdf5r (full granule download, then read)", t = t_hdf5r),
  list(label = "gedidb (Python, GFZ-hosted TileDB query)",     t = t_gedidb)
)
pipelines <- Filter(function(x) !is.null(x$t), pipelines)

tab <- do.call(rbind, lapply(pipelines, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    Pipeline        = x$label,
    `Wall time`     = fmt_s(x$t$seconds_total),
    `Download`      = fmt_s(x$t$seconds_download),
    `Read`          = fmt_s(x$t$seconds_read),
    `Bytes on disk` = fmt_b(x$t$bytes_downloaded),
    Rows            = fmt_int(x$t$n_rows),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
knitr::kable(tab, align = "lrrrrr")
```

```{r speedups, eval = have_data}
lines <- character(0)
bullets <- character(0)
if (!is.null(t_hdf5r) && !is.null(t_sl)) {
  bullets <- c(bullets, sprintf(
    "* spacelaser is %.1f× quicker",
    t_hdf5r$seconds_total / t_sl$seconds_total
  ))
}
if (!is.null(t_hdf5r) && !is.null(t_h5coro)) {
  bullets <- c(bullets, sprintf(
    "* h5coro is %.1f× quicker",
    t_hdf5r$seconds_total / t_h5coro$seconds_total
  ))
}
if (length(bullets) > 0L) {
  lines <- c(lines,
    "### Wall-time ratio vs the download+hdf5r pipeline:",
    "",
    bullets)
}
if (!is.null(t_sl) && !is.null(t_h5coro)) {
  rel <- t_h5coro$seconds_total / t_sl$seconds_total
  lines <- c(lines, "", sprintf(
    "Between the two partial-read pipelines, spacelaser completes **%.2f×** %s h5coro on this workload.",
    if (rel >= 1) rel else 1 / rel,
    if (rel >= 1) "quicker than" else "slower than"
  ))
}
if (length(lines) > 0L) cat("\n", paste(lines, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")

if (!is.null(t_hdf5r) && !is.na(t_hdf5r$seconds_download)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "\n\nDownload+hdf5r phase split: **%s downloading** vs **%s reading** — wall time on this workload is dominated by the file transfer, which is where partial-read pipelines (spacelaser, h5coro) save their time.\n",
    fmt_s(t_hdf5r$seconds_download), fmt_s(t_hdf5r$seconds_read)
  ))
}
```

## Equivalence

```{r equiv, eval = have_data}
if (nrow(equiv) > 0L) {
  tab <- data.frame(
    Pair          = sprintf("%s vs %s", equiv$left, equiv$right),
    `Shared cols` = equiv$n_common_cols,
    `All match`   = ifelse(equiv$all_match, "✓", "✗"),
    Notes         = ifelse(nzchar(equiv$divergent_cols), equiv$divergent_cols, ""),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  knitr::kable(tab, align = "lrll")
} else {
  cat("*Need ≥2 pipelines' data to run the cross-pipeline check.*\n")
}
```

spacelaser and hdf5r agree on every shared column. h5coro matches on
row count and coordinates, and our driver currently shows zeros on
the science columns (rh0–100, quality_flag, sensitivity, elev_*) —
this is a known issue in our h5coro-side code (hyperslice or
fill-value handling), not in h5coro itself; tracked for follow-up.
gedidb is reported alongside rather than inside the equivalence
matrix because it exposes a different slice of the catalogue — see
the next section. Timing comparison remains valid.

## gedidb: a different architecture

gedidb takes a meaningfully different approach to serving GEDI data,
and the comparison below is intended to characterise the differences
rather than adjudicate. A summary of what each pipeline exposes:

| | spacelaser / h5coro / hdf5r | gedidb |
|---|---|---|
| Data source | NASA DAAC granules (live) | Pre-indexed TileDB on GFZ-Potsdam Ceph |
| Auth | NASA Earthdata required | None (public S3) |
| Currency | Always matches NASA's latest processing | Depends on GFZ reprocessing cadence |
| Products | GEDI L1B/L2A/L2B/L4A/L4C + ICESat-2 | L2A/L2B/L4A/L4C only |
| Waveforms (rxwaveform / txwaveform) | Available (L1B) | Not ingested |
| 2D profiles (rh, pai_z, cover_z, pavd_z) | Available | Available |
| `elev_highestreturn` | Native column | Only `_a1`/`_a2` variants |
| Polygon queries | Bbox only | Shapely geometry supported |
| Push-down quality filters | Post-hoc in R | SQL-like kwargs |
| Output | `tibble` / DataFrame | `xarray.Dataset` or DataFrame |

gedidb's timing reflects "query pre-indexed L2A summary metrics from
a European-hosted TileDB", whereas the other pipelines measure
"range-read live NASA granules". Where the products overlap
(L2A/L2B/L4A/L4C, no waveforms), gedidb's TileDB architecture is
latency-efficient by design — the shots are already ingested,
indexed, and filtered — so comparing wall-clock against a live-CMR
pipeline isn't apples-to-apples.

**Row counts reflect different data slices, not equivalent results.**
The other three pipelines return every shot in the bbox (`r fmt_int(t_sl$n_rows)` in
this workload). gedidb returns `r if (!is.null(t_gedidb)) fmt_int(t_gedidb$n_rows) else "—"` rows — the subset that passes
its ingest-time quality filter, which is deliberate and documented
in gedidb's [filter documentation](https://gedidb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/fundamentals.filters.html).

These filters reflect sensible defaults for the canopy-focused
workflows gedidb is primarily designed for, and are clearly
documented. For a like-for-like row comparison, applying the same
filter set to the other pipelines brings them in line. For users
whose work depends on shots that don't pass those filters —
hydrology / water surfaces, urban / coastal research, or anyone
who wants unfiltered raw shots — spacelaser or direct DAAC access
is the path; gedidb is explicit about its filtering choices so
users can pick the approach that fits.

**When each is a great fit:**

- **gedidb** is ideal for pre-cooked summary metrics with SQL-like
  filtering and no auth setup — especially for canopy / vegetation
  work at scale, where the ingest filters reflect community
  practice.
- **spacelaser** is a good fit when you want L1B waveforms,
  ICESat-2 breadth (ATL03/06/07/08/10/13/24 vs ATL08-only via
  icesat2db), shots that fall outside gedidb's default filters, or
  NASA-live reprocessed data.
- **curl + hdf5r (full download + read)** remains a solid choice
  when you want the full granule locally, or when your workflow
  depends on other hdf5r features.

---

See [`README.md`](README.md) for methodology, workload details, variance
discussion, and reproduction instructions.
